jokes.text: I'm glad this holiday only comes every four years.
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on Mother's Day frequency
jokes.punchline: Confusing Mother's Day with leap year occurrence
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer stupidity - perfectly in character mathematical confusion delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: Twenty on the trifecta?
jokes.setup: Family brainstorming what Marge would like for Mother's Day
jokes.punchline: Homer suggests gambling on horse racing as a gift
jokes.explanation: Homer's gambling addiction manifesting as gift suggestion - economical character beat

jokes.text: Her wedding china back?
jokes.setup: Continuing Mother's Day gift suggestions
jokes.punchline: Implies Homer pawned or lost Marge's wedding china
jokes.explanation: Dark implication delivered casually - reveals Homer's past financial irresponsibility

jokes.text: I could get Marge a new window just like this one!
jokes.setup: Homer looking at store window while gift shopping
jokes.punchline: Considers buying the literal window he's looking through
jokes.explanation: Homer's literal-mindedness creating absurd gift idea - solid character comedy

jokes.text: Even Christian videos with talking vegetables.
jokes.setup: Describing what Sprawl-Mart has in stock
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to VeggieTales-style religious content
jokes.explanation: Cultural satire of religious children's content - solid setup for next joke

jokes.text: Mighty Yamses, we are weary of building your food pyramid. Let my pickles go!
jokes.setup: Parodying VeggieTales-style Christian content
jokes.punchline: Moses story with vegetables - 'Yamses' instead of Ramses, pickles instead of people
jokes.explanation: Brilliant layered pun combining biblical story with vegetable wordplay - creative cultural parody

jokes.text: Mmm! Moses.
jokes.setup: Homer watching Christian vegetable video
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature 'mmm' applied to religious figure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer catchphrase applied inappropriately - maximum character integration and economy

jokes.text: How dare you challenge my perceptions of what old people can do!
jokes.setup: Bart surprised Grampa has a job
jokes.punchline: Mock-outrage at his own ageism
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary on ageist assumptions - Bart being unusually self-aware

jokes.text: Like I can't shoplift with my mouth.
jokes.setup: Grampa's shoplifter surveillance comment
jokes.punchline: Bart's creative shoplifting method
jokes.explanation: Creative criminal logic perfectly suited to Bart - original and economical

jokes.text: Oh, look, Jaclyn Smith has her own line of axe heads!
jokes.setup: Marge browsing Sprawl-Mart merchandise
jokes.punchline: Elegant actress endorsing violent tools
jokes.explanation: Absurd celebrity endorsement - good cultural observation about bizarre product lines

jokes.text: only imperfect stuff for me.
jokes.setup: Homer hasn't found perfect gift for Marge
jokes.punchline: But has found imperfect things for himself
jokes.explanation: Homer's selfish shopping priorities revealed with mild self-awareness

jokes.text: Hey, look. It's 'Hairy Ass' Tubman.
jokes.setup: Nelson spotting Homer at the store
jokes.punchline: Harriet Tubman pun based on Homer's appearance
jokes.explanation: Juvenile historical figure pun - fits Nelson's bully character but somewhat cheap

jokes.text: Well, well, if it isn't Fatty and Smell-ma.
jokes.setup: Nelson continuing to mock Homer and Selma
jokes.punchline: Rhyming insults based on their names
jokes.explanation: Basic playground insults - predictable wordplay that fits Nelson but lacks creativity

jokes.text: Your names can't hurt us.
jokes.setup: Homer responding to Nelson's insults with defiance
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction by his pained reaction
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for immediate contradiction - good structure and economy

jokes.text: We got one when we appeared on an episode of The Price is Right, which the network refused to air. Apparently, we're not 'TV pretty.'
jokes.setup: Explaining how they got the Kitchen Carnival
jokes.punchline: Their TV appearance was too ugly to broadcast
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-aware joke for these characters - believable backstory that's both sad and funny

jokes.text: Endorsed by the American Carny Association.
jokes.setup: Kitchen Carnival product description
jokes.punchline: Dubious endorsement from carnival workers
jokes.explanation: Good fake endorsement joke - questions the credibility of carnival food

jokes.text: I never thought I'd say this, but you slags are all right.
jokes.setup: Homer pleased with Patty and Selma's gift suggestion
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment using British slang insult
jokes.explanation: Homer's attempt at reconciliation still includes an insult - perfectly in character

jokes.text: I feel like a secretary on Administrative Professionals Day!
jokes.setup: Selma enjoying the rare positive attention
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to forced workplace appreciation
jokes.explanation: Good observation about artificial appreciation - fits Selma's cynicism

jokes.text: It is so thoughtful, it makes the kids' gifts look like crap.
jokes.setup: Homer praising his own gift choice
jokes.punchline: Insulting his children's efforts in the process
jokes.explanation: Homer's thoughtless bragging at kids' expense - classic selfish behavior

jokes.text: Oh, Marge, get a room!
jokes.setup: Marge expressing gratitude for Homer's gift
jokes.punchline: Bart treating normal affection as excessive PDA
jokes.explanation: Typical teenage reaction to parent affection - economical but predictable

jokes.text: Only my hairdresser knows for sure.
jokes.setup: Marge's hair covered in cotton candy
jokes.punchline: Hair color commercial reference applied to candy-covered hair
jokes.explanation: Good vintage commercial reference applied to visual situation - fits Marge's era

jokes.text: Dare I pour caramel over the cotton candy?
jokes.setup: Marge contemplating extreme sugar combination
jokes.punchline: Treating it as a momentous decision
jokes.explanation: Marge's internal conflict over sugar excess - good setup for her transformation

jokes.text: And then finally, I can eat my shorts!
jokes.setup: Homer planning to pour caramel on his clothes
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of Bart's catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Brilliant literalization of iconic catchphrase - creative callback with perfect setup

jokes.text: This is 85 pounds of tooth-melting sugar. Dig in.
jokes.setup: Homer presenting his sugar creation to the kids
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging its dangerous nature while encouraging consumption
jokes.explanation: Homer's terrible parenting disguised as generosity - specific weight adds absurdity

jokes.text: That's it, kids. Suckle daddy's sugar ball.
jokes.setup: Homer encouraging kids to eat his sugar creation
jokes.punchline: Uncomfortably intimate phrasing for candy consumption
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable phrasing creates cringe humor - works but somewhat cheap

jokes.text: His passenger is just a big piece of candy. I wish mine was.
jokes.setup: Police observing Homer driving with sugar ball as passenger
jokes.punchline: Lou wishing his partner was candy instead of Wiggum
jokes.explanation: Perfect police partner dynamic - Lou's deadpan desire to replace Wiggum with candy

jokes.text: That's my old lady.
jokes.setup: Marge asking if Homer is spooning the sugar ball
jokes.punchline: Homer referring to sugar ball as his romantic partner
jokes.explanation: Homer's misplaced romantic attachment to food object - economical character beat

jokes.text: I was saving sugar for my wedding night!
jokes.setup: Ned crying about the sugar situation
jokes.punchline: Treating sugar consumption as sexual milestone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders repression joke - treating sugar like virginity is brilliantly character-specific

jokes.text: I think you should get rid of her. Then we can be together forever!
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting Homer get rid of the sugar ball
jokes.punchline: Sugar ball speaking as jealous romantic rival
jokes.explanation: Creative personification of Homer's sugar addiction as romantic triangle - original concept

jokes.text: I'll think of you whenever I have a stroke or a heart attack.
jokes.setup: Homer saying goodbye to his sugar ball
jokes.punchline: Promising to remember it during health consequences
jokes.explanation: Dark but honest farewell acknowledging health consequences - good structure and honesty

jokes.text: Well, I guess I don't have to worry anymore about the dangers of smoking.
jokes.setup: Homer about to be killed by a bear
jokes.punchline: Finding silver lining in imminent death
jokes.explanation: Perfect gallows humor - finding positive in life-threatening situation with typical Homer logic

jokes.text: Smooth.
jokes.setup: Homer's anti-smoking comment while being attacked
jokes.punchline: Complimenting his own joke timing
jokes.explanation: Homer appreciating his own wit mid-crisis - maximum economy and character consistency

jokes.text: But seeing you like this is more than I can bear.
jokes.setup: Marge comforting traumatized Homer
jokes.punchline: Unintentional bear pun triggers Homer's PTSD
jokes.explanation: Good pun setup - Marge's innocent word choice triggering Homer's trauma response

jokes.text: The angry president woke up, crawled out of his grave and went back to work.
jokes.setup: News report following Homer's bear trauma
jokes.punchline: Describing Lincoln Memorial as reanimated president
jokes.explanation: Creative description of Lincoln Memorial - good visual concept but disconnected from main story

jokes.text: It's time for another installment of 'Kent's Cowards.'
jokes.setup: News segment introduction
jokes.punchline: Recurring segment publicly shaming cowardly behavior
jokes.explanation: Good media satire - exploitative news segment concept fits Kent's character

jokes.text: I went to Starbucks. But you didn't see that on tape.
jokes.setup: Homer deflecting from bear attack footage
jokes.punchline: Emphasizing mundane activities not caught on camera
jokes.explanation: Homer's defensive deflection strategy - good character moment showing his embarrassment

jokes.text: Not with a gun, but with a camera! That's right, Kent. I often get guns and cameras confused. One time, tragically, at a wedding...
jokes.setup: Hunter explaining how he filmed Homer's attack
jokes.punchline: Admitting to confusing weapons with cameras, implying wedding tragedy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark escalation - innocent gun/camera confusion leading to implied wedding massacre

jokes.text: We'd rather have a live sissy mincing around the house than some dead hero any day.
jokes.setup: Lisa comforting Homer about cowardice
jokes.punchline: Preferring emasculated but alive father
jokes.explanation: Sweet but brutally honest support - Lisa's characteristically direct emotional truth

jokes.text: Choke on your candor!
jokes.setup: Homer responding to Lisa's honest but hurtful support
jokes.punchline: Sophisticated vocabulary applied to strangling action
jokes.explanation: Brilliant vocabulary joke - Homer using sophisticated word while committing simple violence

jokes.text: You can strangle Bart, but you can't strangle your humiliation.
jokes.setup: Homer strangling Bart to deal with embarrassment
jokes.punchline: Lisa's wise observation about misdirected anger
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa wisdom - philosophical truth delivered while accepting family violence as normal

jokes.text: The only thing my dad's afraid of is paying child support.
jokes.setup: Kids comparing their fathers' fears
jokes.punchline: Nelson's dad fears financial responsibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson family dysfunction - deadbeat dad reference fits character background

jokes.text: My dad once beat up six employees at KFC. And he'd been up for three days.
jokes.setup: Milhouse bragging about his father's toughness
jokes.punchline: Sleep deprivation explaining restaurant violence
jokes.explanation: Good Milhouse family weirdness - Kirk's random violence explained by exhaustion

jokes.text: What a delightful practically-based joke.
jokes.setup: Burns responding to Homer's bear fear display
jokes.punchline: Overly formal description of crude prank
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns vocabulary - antiquated formality applied to simple workplace bullying

jokes.text: Oh, God, they're in our food! Teddy Grahams, Gummi Bears!
jokes.setup: Homer's bear paranoia extending to snack foods
jokes.punchline: Treating bear-shaped candy as actual threat
jokes.explanation: Good paranoia escalation - Homer's fear extending logically to bear-themed snacks

jokes.text: I'm an intensive Care Bear.
jokes.setup: Homer asking if character is a Care Bear
jokes.punchline: Medical ICU pun on Care Bear name
jokes.explanation: Excellent pun combining children's character with medical terminology - creative and economical

jokes.text: Why does a bear need a crowbar? I don't like to get my hands dirty.
jokes.setup: Homer questioning bear's tool use
jokes.punchline: Bear with hygiene concerns about violence
jokes.explanation: Absurd bear logic - violent creature with cleanliness standards for its violence

jokes.text: Can I fight human-style by slowly poisoning his environment until he loses his fur and becomes sterile?
jokes.setup: Grampa telling Homer to fight the bear like a man
jokes.punchline: Homer's alternative: environmental destruction as warfare
jokes.explanation: Brilliant environmental satire - Homer preferring slow ecological warfare to direct confrontation

jokes.text: You cowardly Swede!
jokes.setup: Grampa insulting Homer's cowardice
jokes.punchline: Random nationality-based insult
jokes.explanation: Random Grampa insult - fits character's confused geography but lacks sophistication

jokes.text: But how will I find out what you had for lunch in 1928? I have a web site!
jokes.setup: Homer asking how he'll get Grampa's old stories
jokes.punchline: Elderly man with website for historical trivia
jokes.explanation: Good technology/age joke - Grampa embracing internet for mundane personal history

jokes.text: Welcome to my home page. Why don't you visit more often?
jokes.setup: Grampa's website appearance
jokes.punchline: Website guilt-tripping visitors like real Grampa
jokes.explanation: Perfect character extension - Grampa's guilt tactics translated to digital medium

jokes.text: It's the same technology they use to keep tabs on Gary Busey.
jokes.setup: Explaining bear tracking technology
jokes.punchline: Comparing to monitoring erratic celebrity
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity reference - Gary Busey's reputation for erratic behavior making tracking logical

jokes.text: Finally, science has joined forces with revenge.
jokes.setup: Homer learning about bear tracking technology
jokes.punchline: Treating tech as alliance between science and vengeance
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - dramatic framing of simple tracking device as cosmic alliance

jokes.text: weighs more than a Mazda Miata and can tear through a tree like a Jewish mother through self-esteem.
jokes.setup: Describing the dangerous bear
jokes.punchline: Comparing bear's destructive power to stereotypical maternal criticism
jokes.explanation: Creative analogy - bear's physical destruction compared to psychological destruction by stereotype

jokes.text: And I did it all without wasting money on one of those stupid welder's masks.
jokes.setup: Homer's training montage with eye damage
jokes.punchline: Homer's penny-pinching causing obvious injury
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer false economy - saving money on safety equipment while suffering obvious consequences

jokes.text: Available wherever fat men dream.
jokes.setup: Bear Buster 5000 commercial
jokes.punchline: Targeting overweight men's fantasies instead of stores
jokes.explanation: Creative advertising parody - targeting demographic by psychological state rather than location

jokes.text: I know. If I get really scared, I don't want to ruin the suit.
jokes.setup: Marge noting the suit has no rear protection
jokes.punchline: Homer planning for fear-induced defecation
jokes.explanation: Homer's practical cowardice planning - characteristic but relies on cheap scatological humor

jokes.text: What kind of an example would I be if I didn't take revenge on things?
jokes.setup: Marge forbidding Homer from fighting the bear
jokes.punchline: Homer treating revenge as important parental lesson
jokes.explanation: Homer's twisted parental responsibility - teaching children importance of vengeance

jokes.text: Lisa, the point of Moby Dick is be yourself.
jokes.setup: Lisa citing Moby Dick's moral about revenge
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely wrong literary interpretation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer literary misunderstanding - reducing complex revenge tale to simple self-help message

jokes.text: Gentlemen, sometimes a man must put his marriage at risk for reasons that are confusing even to him.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his bear-fighting mission
jokes.punchline: Admitting his own motivations are incomprehensible
jokes.explanation: Surprisingly self-aware Homer philosophy - acknowledging the irrationality of masculine behavior

jokes.text: When I grow up, I'm legally changing my name to Joe Kickass!
jokes.setup: Homer saying Bart must carry on Simpson name
jokes.punchline: Bart's plan to abandon family name for cooler alternative
jokes.explanation: Good Bart rebellion - rejecting family legacy for juvenile tough-guy name

jokes.text: Howdy, y'all. If you don't like my song, I'll spray you with ink!
jokes.setup: Lisa trying on the country-western octopus costume
jokes.punchline: Combining country music with octopus defense mechanism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant costume concept execution - merging musical performance with cephalopod biology

jokes.text: Yeah. We borrowed 'em for the radio.
jokes.setup: Explaining why bear tracker has no batteries
jokes.punchline: Used safety equipment batteries for entertainment
jokes.explanation: Good priority joke - safety equipment sacrificed for 'Afternoon Delight' listening

jokes.text: I don't want my last words to him to be, 'Clip your toenails, they look like Fritos.'
jokes.setup: Marge worrying about Homer's safety
jokes.punchline: Regretting mundane criticism as potential final words
jokes.explanation: Brilliant marital reality - specific, gross domestic criticism as regrettable final memory

jokes.text: Yeah. It almost wrecked the whole trip.
jokes.setup: Bart reporting that Homer was carried off by bear
jokes.punchline: Treating father's endangerment as trip inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart selfishness - father's life-threatening situation framed as vacation disruption

jokes.text: But first, a snack.
jokes.setup: Connor promising to find Homer before bear kills him
jokes.punchline: Prioritizing food over life-saving rescue
jokes.explanation: Good priority joke - dramatic rescue promise immediately undermined by casual hunger

jokes.text: Jeez, I don't know why bear hugs enjoy such a great reputation.
jokes.setup: Homer getting actual hug from bear
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of 'bear hug' phrase proving disappointing
jokes.explanation: Good literal pun - Homer discovering the reality doesn't match the metaphor

jokes.text: Oh, no. The hunter has become the hunter.
jokes.setup: Homer realizing they're being hunted
jokes.punchline: Confused reversal of 'hunter becomes the hunted' phrase
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer phrase confusion - getting dramatic reversal exactly wrong

jokes.text: Wrong again, liberal media.
jokes.setup: Radio reporting Homer missing and presumed mauled
jokes.punchline: Homer dismissing accurate news as biased
jokes.explanation: Good media satire - blaming political bias for factually accurate reporting

jokes.text: Either as hostage or as what we call a 'forest bride.'
jokes.setup: Connor explaining Homer's relationship with the bear
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic reference to bestiality
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with professional euphemism - Connor's matter-of-fact delivery of disturbing concept

jokes.text: It's so easy to condemn, so hard to create.
jokes.setup: Homer struggling to disguise bear from hunters
jokes.punchline: Profound artistic statement about simple disguise problem
jokes.explanation: Homer's unexpected depth - applying artistic philosophy to practical disguise problem

jokes.text: What you said didn't really add much. I know. I just wanted to belong.
jokes.setup: Hunter making redundant threat
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging meaningless contribution, admitting social neediness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-moment - character acknowledging their dialogue's redundancy and social desperation

jokes.text: I know. I just wanted to belong.
jokes.setup: Hunter admitting his comment was unnecessary
jokes.punchline: Vulnerable confession about need for inclusion
jokes.explanation: Touching vulnerable moment in macho context. Good emotional comedy mixing tough guys with basic human needs.

jokes.text: Yeah. But now he's being attacked by an elephant!
jokes.setup: Lisa celebrating bear reaching safety from humans
jokes.punchline: Immediate new threat from different large animal
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure - sanctuary from one threat immediately replaced by another

jokes.text: You set out to fight an animal, and you ended up saving its life.
jokes.setup: Marge reflecting on Homer's adventure outcome
jokes.punchline: Complete reversal of Homer's original violent intention
jokes.explanation: Good ironic observation - Homer's character growth through accidental heroism
